data.name: Help_Description
data.value: Review setup, operations, and troubleshooting procedures.

data.name: Help_LoadFailed
data.value: The operation manual could not be loaded.

data.name: Home_Description
data.value: Review configured sync rules, processing states, and recent conflicts.

data.name: Metric_HeldHelp
data.value: Conflicts that require a manual decision or whose resolution failed.

data.name: Auth_LoginDescription
data.value: Sign in to access sync configuration and operations.

data.name: Auth_SetupRequiredRemote
data.value: The administrator account isn't initialized. Complete setup from localhost on the server.

data.name: Auth_InvalidCredentials
data.value: The user name or password is incorrect.

data.name: Auth_SetupDescription
data.value: Set the password for administrator “admin”. This operation is only available from localhost.

data.name: Auth_LocalOnly
data.value: This operation is only available from localhost on the server.

data.name: Auth_AlreadyInitialized
data.value: The administrator account is already initialized.

data.name: Auth_ResetDescription
data.value: Reset the administrator password and invalidate existing sign-in cookies. Sync settings and history aren't changed.

data.name: Auth_ChangeDescription
data.value: Change the administrator password. Previous sign-in cookies become invalid.

data.name: Auth_ErrorTooShort
data.value: The password must be at least 12 characters.

data.name: Auth_ErrorTooLong
data.value: The password must be no more than 128 characters.

data.name: Auth_ErrorGeneric
data.value: The operation couldn't be completed. Check your input.

data.name: Error_Title
data.value: An error occurred while processing the request

data.name: Error_Description
data.value: Try again later. Contact the administrator if the problem continues.

data.name: Systems_Description
data.value: Register and edit synchronized systems and their business database connections, and pause synchronization during maintenance. Connection details are encrypted in the coordinator database.

data.name: Systems_PauseNote
data.value: While a system is paused, checkpoints for rules involving it do not advance. After resuming, processing catches up from accumulated notifications to the latest state.

data.name: Systems_SystemCodeHelp
data.value: Identifies the system in sync rules and queue read positions. It cannot be changed after registration.

data.name: Systems_DisableHelp
data.value: Turn this off to remove the system from synchronization. For maintenance, use Pause in the system list instead.

data.name: Systems_DbConnectionHelp
data.value: Connection details are stored encrypted. You can also save only the system information without entering a connection.

data.name: Systems_KeepPassword
data.value: Leave blank to keep the current password

data.name: Systems_GlobalPauseHelp
data.value: Releasing the global pause does not resume individually paused systems.

data.name: Systems_AllResumedMessage
data.value: The global pause was released. Individually paused systems remain paused.

data.name: Systems_Saved
data.value: The system and database connection settings were saved.

data.name: Routes_Description
data.value: Configure source and destination systems, sync direction, conflict scope, and resolution policy. A rule can be enabled after deployment to its business databases is verified.

data.name: Routes_Empty
data.value: No sync rules have been configured.

data.name: RouteEdit_DirectionHelp
data.value: Even with bidirectional sync, records created independently at the destination are not synchronized.

data.name: RouteEdit_PolicyHelp
data.value: Conflicts are recorded in history. Configure per-column overrides in table mappings.

data.name: RouteEdit_DeploymentNote
data.value: Save the table mapping, then verify every database configuration under Database deployment. The rule can be enabled after verification succeeds.

data.name: RouteEdit_Saved
data.value: The sync rule settings were saved.

data.name: Conflicts_Description
data.value: Review synchronization conflicts and safely resolve values that were placed on hold.

data.name: ConflictDetail_NotFoundDescription
data.value: The specified conflict does not exist or is outside the retention period.

data.name: ConflictDetail_Empty
data.value: This conflict has no field details.

data.name: Operations_Description
data.value: Review queue checkpoints by system, recent message processing results, and configuration audit history.

data.name: Operations_NoAudits
data.value: No configuration changes have been recorded.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_VerifyHelp
data.value: Verify that every target database contains the tables and triggers required for synchronization.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_DirectDisabled
data.value: Direct deployment from the management UI is unavailable in this environment. Download the SQL and have a DBA review it before execution.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ConfirmedSql
data.value: I reviewed the SQL and target database

data.name: DatabaseSetup_TriggerNote
data.value: Deleting or disabling a rule does not automatically remove its triggers from business databases.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ConfirmationRequired
data.value: Review the SQL and confirm the target database first.

data.name: Mappings_Description
data.value: Map business database tables, columns, and keys for each sync rule, and configure per-column policies, fixed values, and deletion behavior. Saving mappings does not change the business database schema.

data.name: Mappings_EditHelp
data.value: Only systems with a configured database connection can be inspected.

data.name: Mappings_SourceConditionExpressionHelp
data.value: {source} refers to the source table. Only matching records are synchronized. If a synchronized record stops matching, the deletion synchronization settings apply. Existing records are reevaluated on their next change.

data.name: Mappings_ColumnsHelp
data.value: Add sync fields and select the columns and direction for each system. Use a fixed value for destination-only columns.

data.name: Mappings_NoMatchingColumns
data.value: No columns match the current filters.

data.name: Mappings_RelatedTablesHelp
data.value: Configure tables joined directly to the source table. “Use as sync fields” adds related-table columns as sync fields, while “Eligibility only” determines eligibility from the presence of a matching row. When related-table changes are used as a sync trigger, the source record is read again even if only a related row changed.

data.name: Mappings_JoinExpressionHelp
data.value: Reference both {source} and {related}. Prefer equality joins between indexed columns to preserve trigger performance.

data.name: Mappings_ConditionExpressionHelp
data.value: {related} refers to the related table and {source} to the source table. Enter one valid database predicate.

data.name: Mappings_DetectRelatedChanges
data.value: Use related-table changes as a sync trigger

data.name: Mappings_ConfirmEligibilityRemovesFields
data.value: Changing the usage to “Eligibility only” will remove {1} sync field(s) that use columns from related table “{0}”. Continue?

data.name: Mappings_ConfirmRelatedTableRemovesFields
data.value: Deleting related table “{0}” will also remove {1} sync field(s) that use its columns. Continue?

data.name: Mappings_FixedValueKeyHelp
data.value: A fixed-value key is combined with the mapped key columns to identify a destination row. Records created at the destination are not sent to the source.

data.name: Mappings_DeleteSyncHelp
data.value: You can use different deletion methods at the source and destination. Physical deletion stores the previous values in a tombstone. Logical deletion treats an update to the configured deletion value as a delete.

data.name: Mappings_Saved
data.value: The mapping was saved safely and synchronization was paused. Apply and verify the database setup when required, then enable the route.

data.name: Mappings_ConnectionMissing
data.value: The database connection for system '{0}' is not configured. Configure it under Systems.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ResultApplyVerified
data.value: Deployment to {0} was verified. After deploying to every target database, verify the database configuration at the bottom of this page.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ResultApplyUnverified
data.value: The SQL was run on {0}, but the required objects could not be verified.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ResultObjectsMissing
data.value: {0} does not contain all required synchronization objects.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ResultAllVerified
data.value: Synchronization objects were verified in every business database. The rule can now be enabled under Basic settings.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_WarningDdl
data.value: Synchronization tables, triggers, permissions, and related objects will be added to or updated in the business databases. Have a DBA review the SQL before deploying to production.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_WarningDeleteEnabled
data.value: Delete synchronization is enabled. Deletes are synchronized per record and use the normal conflict check when the destination has changed.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_WarningDeleteDisabled
data.value: Delete synchronization is disabled. Physical DELETE operations are not synchronized, and logical deletion changes are processed as normal updates.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_WarningCompositeKey
data.value: Composite keys are represented as a JSON EntityId. Confirm that the combined value is no longer than 256 characters.

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeCreateQueue
data.value: Create SyncChangeQueue if it does not exist

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeCreateAppliedMessage
data.value: Create SyncAppliedMessage if it does not exist

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeCreateEntityOrigin
data.value: Create SyncEntityOrigin if it does not exist

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeCreateDeleteTombstone
data.value: Create SyncDeleteTombstone if it does not exist

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeRecordDeployment
data.value: Record this SQL deployment identifier in SyncCoordinatorDeployment

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeSqlServerPermissions
data.value: Required permissions: CREATE TABLE and CREATE/ALTER TRIGGER, or equivalent DDL permissions

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeMySqlPermissions
data.value: Required permissions: CREATE, TRIGGER, and DROP when updating existing triggers

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangePostgreSqlPermissions
data.value: Required permissions: CREATE on the public and target schemas, and TRIGGER on the target table

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeTrigger
data.value: Create or update the change-detection trigger on {0}.{1}

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangePhysicalDelete
data.value: Store physical DELETE values in the tombstone and detect them as deletes

data.name: DatabaseSetup_ChangeLogicalDelete
data.value: Detect an update that sets {0} to the configured value as a delete

data.name: ConflictDetail_MergeUnavailableHeld
data.value: Held because values could not be merged

data.name: ConflictDetail_DeleteMergeUnavailableHeld
data.value: Deletion held because it cannot be merged

data.name: Webhooks_Description
data.value: Manage notification destinations, synchronization events, and delivery results. Endpoint failures do not stop synchronization.

data.name: Webhooks_HttpWarning
data.value: HTTP does not encrypt the payload or signature. Use HTTPS outside a closed internal network.

data.name: Webhooks_NoSignatureWarning
data.value: Without a signature, the receiver cannot verify the sender.

data.name: Webhooks_RegenerateSecret
data.value: Generate a new signing secret when saving

data.name: Webhooks_SecretOnce
data.value: It is shown only once. Click the field to select the entire value, then configure and store it securely.

data.name: Webhooks_TestQueued
data.value: The test notification was queued for delivery.

data.name: Webhooks_ErrorName
data.value: Name is required and must be no more than 200 characters.

data.name: Webhooks_ErrorUrl
data.value: URL must be an absolute HTTP or HTTPS URL without user information or a fragment.

data.name: Webhooks_ErrorEvents
data.value: Select at least one supported event.

data.name: Webhooks_ErrorNotFound
data.value: The specified notification endpoint does not exist.

data.name: Operations_NoSystemEvents
data.value: No system events have been recorded.

data.name: Operations_EventSynchronizationValueValidationHeld
data.value: Held by value transformation or write validation

data.name: Transform_PassThroughUnmapped
data.value: Write values not in the code mapping unchanged

data.name: Transform_TruncateOverflow
data.value: Trim the end when the value is too long

data.name: Transform_HoldSafe
data.value: Do not write; hold on overflow

data.name: Settings_Description
data.value: Configure synchronization, management database retention, display, and the administrator account.

data.name: Settings_PollingIntervalHelp
data.value: 1–300 seconds. Applied from the next Worker cycle.

data.name: Settings_BatchSizeHelp
data.value: 1–5000 items. Used for synchronization and notification delivery.

data.name: Settings_RetentionHelp
data.value: Values are days; use 0 to retain indefinitely.

data.name: Settings_FailedWebhookHelp
data.value: Days to retain deliveries after retries are exhausted.

data.name: Settings_AcknowledgedEventsHelp
data.value: Unacknowledged events are retained regardless of age.

data.name: Settings_CleanupHelp
data.value: Preview the number of expired rows before running cleanup manually. Automatic cleanup uses the same rules.

data.name: Settings_CleanupConfirmation
data.value: Delete {0} expired rows? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: Settings_ErrorPollingInterval
data.value: Polling interval must be between 1 and 300 seconds.

data.name: Settings_ErrorBatchSize
data.value: Items per cycle must be between 1 and 5000.

data.name: Settings_ErrorCompletedInboxRetention
data.value: Completed inbox retention must be 0 or between 30 and 3650 days.

data.name: Settings_ErrorRetention
data.value: Retention must be 0 or between 1 and 3650 days.

data.name: Settings_LanguageHelp
data.value: Select the language used in this browser. The page updates immediately after selection.

data.name: Metric_ValueTransformationErrorsHelp
data.value: Synchronizations that require review because value transformation or validation failed.

data.name: Metric_AttentionConflictsHelp
data.value: Conflicts that require a manual decision or whose resolution failed.

data.name: ConflictDetail_CustomValueHelp
data.value: Enter text directly, or enter a number, true, false, null, or JSON. Line breaks are preserved as part of a string.

data.name: ConflictDetail_CurrentChangedHelp
data.value: You can resolve against the latest value shown here. If it changes again after submission, the update is not applied and a new decision is required.

data.name: ConflictDetail_PendingHelp
data.value: The resolution was received and will be processed by the Worker in order.

data.name: ConflictDetail_QueuedHelp
data.value: The Worker is verifying the current destination value and applying the resolution. Reload this page shortly.

data.name: ConflictDetail_SupersededHelp
data.value: This history entry can no longer be changed.

data.name: ConflictDetail_DeleteExplanation
data.value: A deletion was received, but the destination record has changed. Choose whether to delete the record or keep its current value.

data.name: ConflictDetail_WaitingForPreviousHelp
data.value: This conflict cannot be changed until its turn. Resolve from the first conflict, or prioritize the latest conflict.

data.name: ConflictDetail_NewerConflictsHelp
data.value: There are {0} later conflicts. After resolving this conflict, the next one will be reevaluated against the updated value.

data.name: ConflictDetail_OlderConflictsHelp
data.value: There are {0} earlier conflicts. Resolving this latest conflict will make the earlier conflicts unavailable.

data.name: ConflictDetail_CustomPlaceholder
data.value: String, number, true, false, null, or JSON

data.name: ConflictDetail_ApplyDelete
data.value: Apply the incoming deletion to the destination

data.name: ConflictDetail_CommentPlaceholder
data.value: Record the reason for the decision or what was verified.

data.name: ConflictDetail_ConfirmMessage
data.value: Queue a resolution that applies the selected values to the destination?

data.name: ConflictDetail_ConfirmLatestMessage
data.value: Resolving this latest conflict will make {0} earlier conflicts unavailable. Continue?

data.name: ConflictDetail_Queued
data.value: The resolution was queued. The Worker will verify the current value before applying it.
